metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'gate health' concretely: specify the exact CLI output field(s), exit code(s), or structured artifact (e.g., JSON gate-result block) that must be recorded on the ticket, and state what constitutes a pass vs. a failure for each policy gate.
evidence: ABSENCE: no definition of gate-health format, expected field values, or exit code that constitutes a pass
'record the observed gate health' is a one-time transition documentation step, not a deterministic pass/fail command
E6 checklist: 'every completion-relevant claim has a concrete proving command/check that produces evidence on success (not "should work")'
findings.finding: 'Record the observed gate health on this ticket' is informal and non-deterministic — no structured definition of what constitutes 'healthy' (expected output, exit code, field values) is provided, so different executors may record different things and both declare the criterion met.
findings.location: Verification section: 'record the observed gate health on this ticket'
scenarios: Executor records 'gates look fine' — another reviewer has no way to verify the claim
Gate health is recorded as 'no errors' even though enforcement was never triggered because dry run bypassed it
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note such as: 'Record the JSON gate-result block from the review-plan and close commands; a passing result must show enforce_plan_material_pins: pass and require_plan_review_for_close: pass for at least one fixture ticket in each gate path.'

evidence: 3 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion that asserts the absence of eager backfill, graph-wide invalidation, new pin command, list-output changes, and stored relation migrations.
evidence: Implementation: 'Verify no eager backfill, graph-wide invalidation event, new pin command, list-output change, or stored relation migration occurs'
No AC bullet asserts these absence properties.
findings.finding: The negative-verification statement 'Verify no eager backfill, graph-wide invalidation event, new pin command, list-output change, or stored relation migration occurs' is described in Implementation but is not reflected in any acceptance criterion, leaving it unverifiable at the AC level.
scenarios: An implementation that introduces one of these prohibited behaviors (e.g., a stored relation migration) would pass all three ACs without triggering a failure.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'No eager backfill, graph-wide invalidation event, new pin command, list-output change, or stored relation migration is introduced, as verified by the non-integration test suite passing unchanged.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify what this story's `tests/golden/config_surface.json` edit adds beyond what deps 75de and 2bbd already commit to that file, or explicitly state that this story is the sole golden updater for both keys (with the dep stories' golden file_impact being aspirational/ordered after this story).
evidence: 75de file_impact: 'tests/golden/config_surface.json — Register verify.enforce_plan_material_pins in the config surface golden.'
2bbd file_impact: 'tests/golden/config_surface.json — Record verify.require_plan_review_for_close.'
145e file_impact: 'tests/golden/config_surface.json — Publish the two supported configuration keys.'
ABSENCE: the plan gives no explanation of what this story's golden update adds beyond what the two deps already deliver
findings.finding: tests/golden/config_surface.json is listed as a file_impact edit target in all three stories — dep 75de (adding verify.enforce_plan_material_pins), dep 2bbd (adding verify.require_plan_review_for_close), AND this dogfood story — but the plan does not acknowledge this overlap or state what incremental change this story makes to the golden after deps have already registered both keys.
findings.location: file_impact — tests/golden/config_surface.json; Implementation — 'Update configuration golden surfaces'
scenarios: If deps 75de and 2bbd each update the golden for their own key (as their file_impact states), this story's golden edit may be a no-op or a redundant re-registration that fails silently.
If instead this story is the single source of truth for both golden entries, the dep stories' golden file_impact claims are misleading and the implementation sequencing is ambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion (or extend AC3) covering the `depends_on`/`blocks` normalization scenario described in Implementation.
evidence: Implementation lists: 'depends_on and blocks normalization'
No AC bullet references normalization of depends_on or blocks relations
findings.finding: The 'depends_on and blocks normalization' scenario described in Implementation has no covering acceptance criterion; it is uncovered work that cannot be verified at acceptance time.
scenarios: An implementer ships this scenario but reviewers cannot verify it passed acceptance because no criterion exists for it.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC3: '…and depends_on/blocks relation normalization are proven through public lifecycle surfaces.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the 'dry run' invokes enforcement gates (and if so, document the expected gate output as pass/fail evidence); if it does not, add a non-dry-run step that exercises at least one enforcement refusal and one enforcement pass with both policies enabled in rebar.toml.
evidence: 'dry run' is unqualified — the plan does not specify whether the dry run exercises enforcement gates or only simulates flow

E6(f): 'when the plan CUTS OVER or DEFAULTS to a new code path, at least one AC EXERCISES that path as it will run in production' — rebar.toml enables both policies, making this an active cutover for the rebar project
Anti-FP exception ('cutover stays behind a non-default opt-in flag') does not apply here because the flag IS being flipped on for this project
findings.finding: The Verification step calls for a 'real review-plan and claim/close dry run on fixture tickets,' but 'dry run' is undefined — it may simulate around enforcement gates rather than invoke them. If the dry run bypasses the newly enabled enforce_plan_material_pins and require_plan_review_for_close policies, the live exercise does not demonstrate that the enabled cutover actually operates as it will in production, leaving a gap in E6(f) coverage for the rebar-project cutover.
findings.location: Verification section: 'Run a real review-plan and claim/close dry run on fixture tickets and record the observed gate health on this ticket.'
scenarios: An executor runs a dry-run that passes by simulating around enforcement and records 'gate: healthy' without the enforcement code ever executing — the plan is declared done but the policies are never proven to work
A dry run that does NOT invoke the enforcement gate satisfies the verification step on its face while the actual policy behavior is untested
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'dry run' with 'live execution against fixture tickets (no --dry-run flag) with both policies enabled, capturing the gate decision output (pass/reject) as structured evidence recorded on this ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/release-notes.md` to file_impact (or explicitly confirm that enabling project-level enforcement flags does not meet the 'agent-visible contract change' bar for that file).
evidence: docs/release-notes.md preamble: 'Agent-visible contract changes, newest first. rebar shares one origin/tickets across many clients, so contract changes are called out here when they could be observed by an agent or a different rebar version.'
CHANGELOG.md is in file_impact; docs/release-notes.md is absent from file_impact

Enabling verify.enforce_plan_material_pins and verify.require_plan_review_for_close in rebar.toml is an agent-visible configuration surface change that alters gate behavior for all agents using this project
findings.finding: docs/release-notes.md is an unenumerated consumer: the plan says 'Update configuration golden surfaces and release notes as required by project conventions' but file_impact names only CHANGELOG.md, omitting docs/release-notes.md which is the project's distinct agent-visible contract changelog.
findings.location: Implementation — 'Update configuration golden surfaces and release notes'; file_impact
scenarios: An implementer following only file_impact skips docs/release-notes.md; downstream agents or rebar version consumers that rely on release-notes.md miss the enforcement-flag activation notice.
findings.suggested_fix: Add {"path": "docs/release-notes.md", "reason": "Record activation of verify.enforce_plan_material_pins and verify.require_plan_review_for_close as agent-visible policy changes for this project."} to file_impact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion or verification step covering the configuration golden-surface update and release notes.
evidence: Implementation states: 'Update configuration golden surfaces and release notes as required by project conventions'
No AC mentions golden surfaces or release notes.
findings.finding: The deliverable 'Update configuration golden surfaces and release notes as required by project conventions' in Implementation has no covering acceptance criterion.
scenarios: These outputs could be omitted or incorrect without any AC catching the gap.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Verification: 'Confirm configuration golden surfaces are updated and release notes entry is present.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that sibling stories delivering `verify.enforce_plan_material_pins` and `verify.require_plan_review_for_close` (75de-02b0-d454-410b, 2bbd-87f6-8765-4b00) are closed/merged before this plan's Implementation step is attempted, and add an explicit `depends_on` to make this ordering a hard gate.
evidence: src/rebar/_config_schema.py VerifyConfig dataclass (lines 211–328): no enforce_plan_material_pins or require_plan_review_for_close field
_SECTIONS['verify'] dict (lines 571–589): neither key present as a coercer entry
tests/golden/config_surface.json config_keys array: neither 'verify.enforce_plan_material_pins' nor 'verify.require_plan_review_for_close' listed
rebar.toml [verify] section: neither key present in current project config
Absence: grep of 'enforce_plan_material_pins' and 'require_plan_review_for_close' in src/ returns zero matches
findings.finding: The plan asserts both `verify.enforce_plan_material_pins` and `verify.require_plan_review_for_close` can be enabled in `rebar.toml` and that 'library defaults remain false in the reusable configuration schema' — implying both keys already exist in VerifyConfig and _SECTIONS['verify']. Grep of src/rebar/_config_schema.py confirms neither key is defined in VerifyConfig, neither appears in _SECTIONS['verify'], and tests/golden/config_surface.json lists neither. Both are deliverables of sibling stories (75de-02b0, 2bbd-87f6), not pre-existing schema fields. This plan cannot enable them in rebar.toml until those sibling stories ship.
findings.location: Implementation — 'Enable verify.enforce_plan_material_pins and verify.require_plan_review_for_close in rebar.toml; retain false defaults in the reusable configuration schema.'
scenarios: If an executor reads 'Enable in rebar.toml' as a simple config-file edit, the config loader will warn 'unknown key' and silently ignore both flags until the schema changes ship from the sibling stories.
The AC 'Rebar enables both policies while library defaults remain off' cannot be verified without the schema fields existing.
findings.suggested_fix: Add explicit `depends_on: [75de-02b0-d454-410b, 2bbd-87f6-8765-4b00]` to the ticket and state in the Implementation: 'These sibling stories must be closed first; their schema additions to VerifyConfig and _SECTIONS["verify"] are the prerequisite for the rebar.toml edits here.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that all infrastructure required by the named lifecycle scenarios (PlanMaterialPin, PlanRelationSnapshot, reviewed_related_material, derive_plan_material_pin_health, DerivedPlanReviewHealth, legacy-unpinned status) has shipped from sibling stories before marking any of the three ACs as verifiable, and document this in the plan's Prerequisites or Verification section.
evidence: Grep of 'PlanMaterialPin' in src/: no matches
Grep of 'PlanRelationSnapshot' in src/: no matches
Grep of 'reviewed_related_material' in src/: no matches
Grep of 'derive_plan_material_pin_health' in src/: no matches
Grep of 'DerivedPlanReviewHealth' in src/: no matches
Grep of 'legacy unpinned' in src/: no matches
tests/interfaces/lifecycle/ contains no test file referencing material_pin or plan_material
findings.finding: The lifecycle scenarios named in the Implementation ('legacy unpinned attestations', 'direct child drift', 'depends_on and blocks normalization', 'natural A-to-B-to-C transitivity', 'successful unchanged re-review stopping propagation', 'execution-time self-edit', 'in_progress-to-open reset', 'code-only drift at close', 'deleted and archived pinned targets', 'concurrent review/close changes') all depend on symbols — PlanMaterialPin, PlanRelationSnapshot, reviewed_related_material, derive_plan_material_pin_health, DerivedPlanReviewHealth — that do not exist anywhere in src/. Grep of src/ for all five symbols returns zero matches. These are deliverables of sibling stories acaf-bb4d-9eba-470c and 75de-02b0-d454-410b.
findings.location: Implementation — scenario list; AC2 'End-to-end tests prove direct and natural-transitive invalidation'; AC3 'Execution adaptation, return-to-open reset, code-only close freshness, toggles, deletion/archive handling, concurrency rechecks, and force-close are proven'
scenarios: An executor attempting to write the listed lifecycle scenarios will find none of the required primitives available — every scenario that exercises pin health, drift, or relation normalization will fail to compile or run.
AC1 ('a pre-feature unpinned attestation continues to pass both gates') requires the legacy-unpinned status classification from DerivedPlanReviewHealth, which does not exist.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit precondition to the Implementation section stating that `enforce_plan_material_pins` and `require_plan_review_for_close` must be present in `VerifyConfig` and `_SECTIONS['verify']` (and `config_surface.json`) before or in the same atomic merge as the `rebar.toml` enablement, to avoid `AttributeError` in `gate_enabled()` or silent fail-open from the unknown-key TOML path.
evidence: VerifyConfig in src/rebar/_config_schema.py has no enforce_plan_material_pins or require_plan_review_for_close field
_SECTIONS['verify'] in _config_schema.py has no enforce_plan_material_pins or require_plan_review_for_close coercer
config_surface.json has no entry for either key
gate_enabled() in src/rebar/_commands/gates.py line 49: `return bool(getattr(load_config(cfg_root).verify, attr))` — AttributeError on a missing field is not caught by the `except ConfigError` clause
Plan says 'retain false defaults in the reusable configuration schema' but does not state as a precondition that the keys must exist in VerifyConfig before this story's rebar.toml flip takes effect
findings.finding: The plan enables `enforce_plan_material_pins` and `require_plan_review_for_close` in `rebar.toml` without asserting that these keys must exist in `VerifyConfig` and `_SECTIONS['verify']` before or simultaneously with this story shipping. As of the current codebase, neither key exists in the config schema. The existing `gate_enabled()` calls `getattr(load_config(cfg_root).verify, attr)` — if `attr` is absent from `VerifyConfig`, this raises `AttributeError` (not `ConfigError`), which propagates past the `except ConfigError` clause and produces an unhandled exception at the claim/close call site. Enabling the `rebar.toml` lines before the sibling stories' `VerifyConfig` fields land means the two new gates either break noisily or (if the unknown-key warning path runs first) silently have no effect.
findings.location: Implementation — 'Enable verify.enforce_plan_material_pins and verify.require_plan_review_for_close in rebar.toml'
scenarios: Enabling rebar.toml before sibling-story VerifyConfig fields land → gate_enabled() raises AttributeError at claim/close time → unhandled exception blocks all claims/closes noisily
If the unknown-key TOML warning path runs first → TOML entries silently ignored → both gates behave as if disabled despite rebar.toml saying true
findings.suggested_fix: Add a precondition note: 'This story's rebar.toml change must be merged only after (or atomically with) the sibling story that adds enforce_plan_material_pins and require_plan_review_for_close to VerifyConfig and _SECTIONS["verify"] — the gate_enabled() call raises AttributeError (not ConfigError) on a missing VerifyConfig field, and the unknown-key TOML warning path silently ignores unrecognised keys, so either race condition leaves both gates non-functional.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale in the Implementation section explaining why enabling both policies simultaneously is safe (e.g., 'legacy unpinned attestations pass both gates by design, and require_plan_review_for_close only gates NEW close attempts after enablement, so no existing open ticket requires backfill').
evidence: Plan states 'Enable verify.enforce_plan_material_pins and verify.require_plan_review_for_close in rebar.toml' with no rationale for simultaneous vs. staged enablement
Plan says 'without requiring migration or backfill' as the safety argument, but does not explain why no existing attestation will be invalidated by require_plan_review_for_close
Absence of a positive rationale for 'why both now, why this approach' as required by G6 criterion 4
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for why enabling both policies in `rebar.toml` now — at this story's milestone — is the right moment and approach, rather than, for example, enabling them only after a full-suite dry run on fixture tickets confirms zero unexpected gate activations. The Verification section does describe a dry run ('Run a real review-plan and claim/close dry run on fixture tickets and record the observed gate health on this ticket'), but the Implementation section provides no affirmative justification for why this approach (flip both flags simultaneously, rely on legacy-unpinned-passes-both-gates guarantee) is safe for a live project repo rather than a staged single-flag rollout.
scenarios: An implementer uncertain whether pre-feature tickets carry the right attestation shape for the close gate has no documented basis for confidence that the simultaneous flip is safe
